mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side resilience, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the mechanism reverses direction during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 3.5%, a simultaneous increase in the federal funds rate forces markets to price in prolonged restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for yield curve slope.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, providing a durable earnings tailwind that translates to positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >60% of observations, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0% signals severe economic contraction, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in >50% of sub-1% GDP growth quarters, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from random noise.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a synchronized economic expansion where output growth outpaces labor slack, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or the predictive signal breaks down when inflation exceeds 4%.
